regimeNotes: REGIME CONFIRMED NEUTRAL. June 17 FOMC: Warsh held rates at 3.50-3.75% as expected (97% probability). KEY DEVELOPMENTS: (1) Dot plot updated — eliminated the single projected rate cut from March, signaling rates on hold through year-end; (2) Warsh hawkish press conference — stepped back from Powell-era forward guidance, framed May CPI at 4.2% YoY as still too elevated; (3) Three FOMC members projecting rate hikes in 2026, market pricing 56 pct hike probability by December; (4) Market reaction: S&P 500 -0.6 pct to 7,511.35, Nasdaq -1.2 pct (tech/growth selloff on rate sensitivity), Dow +0.6 pct (defensive rotation). SPY closed $750.33. Crypto: BTC -2.7 pct to $64,774, ETH -3.1 pct to $1,761.98, SOL -3.6 pct to $72.18. Fear & Greed 22 (Extreme Fear). BTC dominance 56.12 pct, total mcap $2.32T (-1.87 pct 24h). Gold slightly softer post-FOMC ($395.00 GLD) but structurally supported by soft DXY (~99.5) and central bank buying. 10Y yield ~4.43-4.50 pct (slightly higher post-FOMC). TLT original easing-cycle thesis invalidated — now held as Dalio-style portfolio ballast only. PCE inflation data due June 25 is next key catalyst for regime reassessment. 0 trades today — all positions hold, no invalidation levels breached, crypto sleeve at exactly 20 pct neutral cap, cash at 19.93 pct NAV.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta in confirmed neutral regime. S&P 500 -0.6 pct to $750.33 on FOMC day (June 17); tech sold off (Nasdaq -1.2 pct) but defensives rotated in (Dow +0.6 pct). SPY at $750.33 (+1.16 pct above $741.75 avgCost). Holding as diversified equity anchor; consumer tailwind from lower oil (Iran deal) partially intact.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward multiple hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

MSFT.thesis: AI/cloud quality compounder at $393.08 (+0.60 pct above $390.74 avgCost). Nasdaq -1.2 pct post-FOMC, MSFT range $390.69-$398.39. Strong FCF and AI/cloud moat intact; holding through hawkish Fed environment as a quality growth anchor.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality cash machine at $373.25 (+3.77 pct above $359.68 avgCost). Showing resilience — rose from $370.90 (June 16) to $373.25 (June 17). Near-monopoly search business provides earnings resilience even in a hawkish rate environment.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value performing well in rotation to $410.30 (+0.44 pct above $408.52 avgCost). Dow +0.6 pct on FOMC day shows defensive-healthcare demand. Healthcare earnings provide inflation-era resilience. Analyst consensus targets 450-466 USD.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

TLT.thesis: Portfolio ballast at $85.80 (avgCost $85.77, essentially flat). Original easing-cycle thesis invalidated by Warsh FOMC — no cuts through 2026, potential hikes. New thesis: Dalio-style tail hedge for growth-disappointment scenario; TLT rallies if economy rolls over. 10Y yield ~4.43-4.50 pct, still far from 5 pct hard invalidation. Watch 4.8 pct as early warning.
TLT.invalidationLevel: 10Y yield decisively above 5 pct (Fed forced to hike again and sustained). 4.8 pct is early warning trigger for reassessment.

GLD.thesis: Debasement and tail-risk hedge at $395.00 (avgCost $386.54, +2.19 pct). Slightly softer post-FOMC hawkishness but structural bid intact: soft DXY (~99.5), geopolitical premium, and central bank buying. Neutral/hawkish Fed is mildly gold-negative near-term but fiscal concerns and uncertainty support it.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with a strong USD reversal; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,774 (avgCost $64,629, +0.22 pct). Fell 2.7 pct on FOMC day. Fear & Greed 22 (Extreme Fear), BTC dominance 56.12 pct, total mcap $2.32T. Crypto sleeve at exactly 20 pct NAV (neutral-regime cap). HOLD through hawkish Fed — Extreme Fear contrarian signal preserved for next risk-on leg.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,761.98 (avgCost $1,667.47, +5.67 pct). Fell 3.1 pct on FOMC day. ETH dominance still historically low; asymmetric upside when risk-on regime returns. Hold existing position in neutral regime.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $72.18 (avgCost $67.81, +6.44 pct above cost). Fell 3.6 pct on FOMC day. Trimmed June 17 morning to comply with neutral-regime 20 pct crypto sleeve cap. Remaining 20.3220 SOL (~1.45 pct NAV) is small optionality; hold for narrative upside when regime turns risk-on.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
